mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals a tight labor market that drives wage growth and consumer spending, which directly supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding residential mortgage debt indicate synchronized credit expansion and housing demand, which historically precede positive S&P 500 equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint momentum of HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 52 percent in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production exceeding 2.0 percent confirms rapid capacity utilization recovery, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns following INDPRO spikes above 2.0 percent are statistically indistinguishable from zero (95% confidence interval includes zero) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly expands the aggregate corporate revenue base, mechanically supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all available decades.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month forces anticipated monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL accelerates above the 0.5 percent threshold over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that drives consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 60-day forward returns, with a directional accuracy rate below 53 percent over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a sharp decline in residential mortgage debt exceeding 5 percent over two months signals credit contraction, which rapidly dampens consumer demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns following RSAFS contractions above 5 percent are not significantly negative (t-stat > -1.5) over a 10-year out-of-sample evaluation.
